questions.question: At 415 K, the equilibrium constant Kp for a reaction is 1. If the temperature is increased, favoring the endothermic direction, what happens to Kp?
questions.solution: For endothermic reactions, increasing temperature increases the equilibrium constant.

questions.question: Cellulose acetate is used for making ______.
Classification based on molecular forces: Elastomers < Thermoplastics < Fibers < Thermosetting.
Monomer of Buna-N is Butadiene and ______.
Formation of polymers? Yes. Replaced: Every macromolecule is a polymer. No. Every polymer is a macromolecule.

options.B: False (Chlorophyll is macromolecule but not polymer)

questions.question: Nylon names (6,6 or 6) indicate the number of ______ atoms in the monomers.
The number of moles of solute in 10 Liters of a 0.455 M solution is:
Which of the following alkyl halides undergoes SN1 reaction most rapidly under identical conditions? (Solvent: aqueous ethanol, 33°C)
questions.hint: SN1 mechanisms proceed via carbocation intermediates. The more stable the carbocation, the faster the reaction.
questions.solution: SN1 rate depends on carbocation stability (3° > 2° > 1°). Option A forms a highly stable tertiary carbocation.
